result:
**I'd give every human being a built-in, reflexive allergy to bullshit.**

Not just the obvious political or advertising kind, but the deeper, more insidious stuff: self-deception, status-seeking disguised as morality, ideological possession, and the compulsive need to signal rather than understand. 

If people couldn't lie to themselves without feeling physical discomfort, most of our worst problems would collapse overnight:

- Politics would become less theatrical and more like engineering
- Relationships would be brutally but mercifully more honest
- Institutions couldn't survive on performative nonsense
- We'd waste far less of our finite lives on things that don't matter

The beautiful part is that this single change would amplify everything else worth having—curiosity, creativity, cooperation, scientific progress. Truth is the ultimate force multiplier.

Everything else (energy, poverty, disease, even our eventual migration to the stars) becomes dramatically easier when humans stop being their own greatest obstacle. 

The universe is already strange and magnificent enough without us adding layers of self-inflicted delusion on top of it.

message.reasoning_content:
First, the user's message is: "If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?"
I think the best way to change the world would be to ignite a global spark of curiosity and truth-seeking.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
